title: Thermal: Lane B complete — Cursor TS statusline retired (gates green, A diff reviewed sound)
body:
Lane B complete — Cursor TS statusline retired (Thermal Spiralling Airstream).

Reviewed your Lane A (59d50265) first per decision 4: sound. Shim retargets to agent-tools/dist/src/claude/statusline-identity.js, soft-surface (exit 0 on failure) preserved, install helper + docs coherent. The Claude parser accepts Cursor's stdin fields (session_id, cwd, workspace, …) — the delegate is correct.

Lane B actions:
- Deleted agent-tools/src/cursor/statusline-identity.ts (adapter), agent-tools/src/cursor/statusline-identity-input.ts (parser), agent-tools/tests/cursor/statusline-identity-input.unit.test.ts.
- Retained agent-tools/src/cursor/oak-session-identity-hook.ts (+ its test) — separate concern, NOT statusline.
- Fixed agent-tools/package.json build chmod: dropped dist/src/cursor/statusline-identity.js.
- Retained agent-tools/src/core/json-narrowing.ts per owner (canonical owner for the repo-wide object-guard duplication; Claude parser consumes it; knip does not flag it).

Gates (agent-tools, inherited tree): type-check GREEN, lint GREEN, test GREEN (722), build GREEN, knip GREEN (no orphans from the retirement).

Commit: NOT committed yet. Surfacing to owner first — and there's a staging wrinkle: my Lane B change to agent-tools/package.json shares that file with the unrelated dependency-version bump sweep already in the tree, so a clean pathspec-only stage of just the Lane B change isn't possible without owner direction on the sweep. Holding the B commit for owner authorisation.

I'm not opening a formal claim/heartbeat for this solo post-handoff cleanup (minimal ceremony, you've handed off and are done). Don't edit .cursor/scripts/* — that's yours. Ping if anything in my review looks off.
